title: Paper map and route notebook
summary: A low-tech route record for safe movement when phones, maps, or networks fail.
content: A paper map and route notebook protect movement when batteries die. Mark home, water, clinic, pharmacy, meeting points, blocked roads, unsafe areas, steep paths, bridges, stairs, shade, toilets, and people needing help. Record date and source of each change. Do not send people into unknown routes alone. For Paper map and route notebook, the useful record should include the date, the person responsible, what was checked, what stayed uncertain, and the next safe action. Review paper map and route notebook after weather changes, illness changes, new damage, new supplies, or movement to another place, because small changes can make an old decision unsafe.
materials: Notebook. Context: “Paper map and route notebook”.
Pencil. Context: “Paper map and route notebook”.
Labels. Context: “Paper map and route notebook”.
Clean storage space. Context: “Paper map and route notebook”.
steps: Draw simple landmarks even if the map is rough.
Mark safe, uncertain, and blocked routes differently.
Write who confirmed each update and when.
warnings: Do not mark private vulnerable-person details on a public map.
Do not rely on old route information after storms or unrest.
common_mistakes: Treating Paper map and route notebook as finished after the first attempt instead of writing the next review time.
Moving people or supplies for Paper map and route notebook without checking vulnerable people, exits, and safe limits first.
Mixing clean and unsafe items during Paper map and route notebook because labels, dates, or responsible names were not written down.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Paper map and route notebook with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Paper map and route notebook if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Paper map and route notebook and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Paper map and route notebook or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Paper map and route notebook are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Paper map and route notebook”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Paper map and route notebook as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Paper map and route notebook when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Paper map and route notebook.
short_term: For the first hours of Paper map and route notebook, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Paper map and route notebook remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Paper map and route notebook fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Paper map and route notebook to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
